columns.extraction_plan: The add-on treatment is docetaxel. This is evident from the title "Chemohormonal Therapy in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer: Long-Term Survival Analysis of the Randomized Phase III E3805 CHAARTED Trial" and the abstract which mentions "ADT in combination with docetaxel". Also, the purpose states "Docetaxel added to androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) significantly increases the longevity of some patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer."
The median age was 64 years (range, 36 to 88 years) in the combination arm - Extract "64" from the text.
versus 63 years (range, 39 to 91 years) in the ADT alone arm. - Extract "63" from the text.

The study abstract states that 790 patients were equally randomized to receive either ADT in combination with docetaxel 75 mg/m² for up to six cycles or ADT alone. This implies that approximately half of the patients (397) were assigned to the docetaxel arm. The full number and percentage is not explicitly stated in the abstract, but can be calculated from the total number of patients and the study design. Page 4 states that 397 patients were in the ADT plus docetaxel arm. Page 4 also states that 263 patients (66.2%) in the combination arm had high-volume disease.
The control arm received ADT alone. Therefore, the number and percentage of patients receiving docetaxel in the control arm is 0.

columns.extraction_plan: The text states, "289 patients (72.8%) in the combination arm versus 286 patients (72.8%) in the ADT alone arm had had no PLT and were considered to have de novo metastatic disease." Since de novo metastatic disease is equivalent to synchronous metastases, we can extract 289 (72.8%) for the treatment arm (ADT plus docetaxel).

In the Introduction section (pages 1-2), the text mentions that combining novel therapies with ADT at the time of initiating systemic therapy for mHSPC has emerged as a strategy to potentially delay the development of CRPC and improve quality of life and OS. This indicates that quality of life was considered as a factor in the study. However, the results of the quality of life are not reported in the provided text. Therefore, the answer is "Yes, but results not reported".

columns.extraction_plan: The treatment arms are 'ADT in combination with docetaxel' and 'ADT alone'. These are mentioned in the abstract and introduction sections.

The treatment regimen for Arm 1 is reported in multiple locations within the text. Specifically, it is described as 'ADT plus docetaxel'.

*   **Page 1, Abstract:** '...either ADT in combination with docetaxel 75 mg/m² for up to six cycles...'
*   **Page 3, Procedures:** '...randomly assigned at a one-to-one ratio to ADT alone versus ADT plus docetaxel at a dose of 75 mg/m² every 3 weeks for up to six cycles without daily prednisone.'

To extract the value, look for instances of 'ADT plus docetaxel' or 'androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) in combination with docetaxel'.

The study investigates "Chemohormonal Therapy Versus Androgen Ablation Randomized Trial for Extensive Disease in Prostate Cancer". This indicates two treatment classes: chemohormonal therapy (ADT + docetaxel) and androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) alone.
Extract the trial name from the title of the article: "E3805 CHAARTED Trial".

The "Type of Therapy" is described throughout the text. The study compares "ADT (androgen-deprivation therapy) in combination with docetaxel" versus "ADT alone" for metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer (mHSPC). The specific docetaxel regimen (75 mg/m^2 every 3 weeks for up to six cycles) is also mentioned in the abstract and methods section.
